playbook.intent: Approaching audit window → control evidence current, gaps closed, walkthrough rehearsed, every exception logged with owner + date.
playbook.scope: Annual SOC2 / GDPR audit deadline approaches. The team brings each required control to current state: every artifact is tagged in the repo, every secret has a verified custodian, the supply chain is provably scanned, and the auditor walkthrough is scripted and rehearsed. Exceptions are logged with remediation owners and dates. Output is an audit binder ready to ship.

success-criteria.criterion: Control-to-artifact map exists and resolves for every control
Secrets inventory current with named custodian per secret
Supply-chain scan green within last 30 days
Open exceptions logged with owner + remediation date
Vendor risk assessment current for all in-scope vendors

stage.name: Inventory + gap analysis
stage.intent: Map controls to repo artifacts; find gaps.

tasks.task: Pull current vs gap-analysis vs target via strategy-analysis docs
Run process-mining where automation gates exist
Score each control as green / yellow / red

stage.decision-gate: Advance when every control has either a green status or a documented gap with owner.

stage.name: Close gaps + supply chain
stage.intent: Trivy / CodeQL / container / IaC scans + license compliance run + remediated.
stage.decision-gate: Advance when scan output is green within 30 days and all P0 supply-chain risks have remediation owners.

stage.name: Process + governance evidence
stage.intent: Approval-token JWT + conventional-commits + AI code gate trail = auditable engineering controls.

tasks.task: Verify approval-token JWT flow on privileged ops
Walk EU AI Act compliance applicability

stage.decision-gate: Advance when every privileged action has a JWT trail and there are no untagged commits on protected branches.

stage.name: Walkthrough + exception ledger
stage.intent: Rehearse the auditor walkthrough; log exceptions with owners + dates.
stage.decision-gate: Required output: rehearsed walkthrough + signed exception ledger. No 'we'll wing it'.

extra.value: [Choosing the auditor / commercial procurement — handled by Legal/Finance, EU AI Act / sector-specific compliance beyond SOC2 + GDPR — covered separately, Initial certification — assumes recurring-audit cadence; first-time audits add scope]
[Last year's audit report + remediation list, List of in-scope systems documented, Approval-token signed-JWT in place for privileged operations, Renovate/Trivy + SBOM tooling wired]

[Discovering gaps two weeks before the audit — stress + cut corners, Exception ledger without remediation dates — auditor flags as evergreen, 'Walkthrough rehearsed only on slides, not against the live repo', Vendor risk assessment skipped on 'small' SaaS — auditors find them anyway]

['Can I point at the repo artifact for every required control in <30 seconds?', 'Do all secrets have named custodians who would answer a midnight page?', 'Did we rehearse the walkthrough against the actual repo state?']
{human: faion get-content soc2-gdpr-audit-prep --format md, agent: faion get-content soc2-gdpr-audit-prep --format context, json: faion get-content soc2-gdpr-audit-prep --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: Initial draft for F-064 P6 product-dev team persona}]
